contract.title: Will the SEC approve a spot Solana ETF in 2025?
contract.resolution_criteria:
Resolves YES if the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission approves at least one
spot Solana exchange-traded fund (an ETF holding SOL tokens directly, not futures-based)
for listing on a U.S. national securities exchange (NYSE Arca, Cboe BZX, or NASDAQ)
with an effective approval order issued on or before 2025-12-31. Resolves NO otherwise.

conditions.label: SEC approves spot SOL ETF on NYSE Arca
conditions.summary: By 2025-12-31, SEC issues an effective order approving a NYSE Arca rule change to list and trade a spot Solana ETF (holding SOL directly, not futures).

conditions.label: SEC approves spot SOL ETF on Cboe BZX
conditions.summary: By 2025-12-31, SEC issues an effective order approving a Cboe BZX rule change to list and trade a spot Solana ETF (holding SOL directly, not futures).

conditions.label: SEC approves spot SOL ETF on NASDAQ
conditions.summary: By 2025-12-31, SEC issues an effective order approving a NASDAQ rule change to list and trade a spot Solana ETF (holding SOL directly, not futures).

contract.narrative: Through 2025, the SEC sent mixed signals: enforcement actions and custody cautions (e.g., Sept. 30 statements) dampened odds, while Sept. 17 approval of generic listing standards and inter‑agency harmonization modestly improved prospects. Procedural and personnel shifts in Investment Management offered little clarity. The year ended without a spot SOL ETF approval, so the market resolved No.

timeline.title: Final Rule Delegation of Authority to the Director of the Division of Investment Management IA-6934
timeline.regulator: U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission — Division of Investment Management
timeline.one_line_why: Delegation lets the IM director act on fund registration/exemptive matters, slightly increasing odds a spot SOL ETF clears IM hurdles before 2025-12-31.

timeline.title: Poking Holes: Statement in Response to No-Action Relief for State Trust Companies Acting as Crypto Asset Custodians
timeline.regulator: Securities and Exchange Commission
timeline.one_line_why: SEC statement calling out problems with no-action custody relief signals regulatory caution on crypto custody, lowering odds of timely spot SOL ETF approval.

timeline.title: Cultivating Confidence: The Role of Custody in Institutional Confidence – Public Trust and Oversight; Remarks before the Digital Assets Summit Singapore
timeline.regulator: Securities and Exchange Commission
timeline.one_line_why: SEC speech emphasizing custody and institutional confidence reduces a key regulatory hurdle, modestly increasing odds of spot SOL ETF approval.

timeline.title: Agenda, Panelists for SEC-CFTC Roundtable on Regulatory Harmonization Efforts Announced
timeline.regulator: Commodity Futures Trading Commission
timeline.one_line_why: CFTC‑SEC harmonization roundtable signals regulatory coordination but provides no direct indication that the SEC will approve a spot SOL ETF in 2025.

timeline.title: CFTC and SEC Extend Form PF Compliance Date to Oct. 1, 2026
timeline.regulator: Commodity Futures Trading Commission
timeline.one_line_why: Extending Form PF reporting to Oct 2026 affects private‑fund compliance timing but has minimal, indirect relevance to SEC approvals of a spot Solana ETF by end of 2025.

timeline.title: SEC Approves Generic Listing Standards for Commodity-Based Trust Shares
timeline.regulator: Securities and Exchange Commission — Division of Trading and Markets
timeline.one_line_why: SEC approved generic listing standards for commodity-based trust shares, easing approval path and increasing odds (and speeding) of a Cboe-listed spot SOL ETF.

timeline.title: CFTC and SEC Issue Joint Statement on Regulatory Harmonization Efforts
timeline.regulator: Commodity Futures Trading Commission
timeline.one_line_why: Joint SEC–CFTC harmonization reduces crypto regulatory uncertainty, modestly increasing odds the SEC will approve a spot SOL ETF in 2025.

timeline.title: Joint Statement from the Chairman of the SEC and Acting Chairman of the CFTC
timeline.regulator: U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission
timeline.one_line_why: Joint SEC/CFTC statement signals regulatory stance on crypto but contains no direct ETF approval action; ambiguous effect on SOL spot ETF odds.

timeline.title: SEC and CFTC Issue Joint Statement on Regulatory Harmonization Efforts; Will Co-Host Roundtable Sept. 29
timeline.regulator: Securities and Exchange Commission
timeline.one_line_why: General SEC–CFTC harmonization roundtable is background policy news and does not directly affect approval odds for a spot SOL ETF in 2025.

timeline.title: On the Road: SEC Crypto Task Force to Host a Series of Roundtables Across the U.S.
timeline.regulator: Securities and Exchange Commission
timeline.one_line_why: SEC crypto roundtables are general outreach and provide background on policy engagement but do not materially change odds of a spot SOL ETF approval.

timeline.title: Acting Chairman Pham Lauds Presidential Working Group Recommendations to Usher in Golden Age of Crypto in the U.S.
timeline.regulator: Commodity Futures Trading Commission
timeline.one_line_why: CFTC praise of PWG pro-crypto recommendations modestly raises regulatory support for crypto, slightly increasing odds the SEC approves a spot SOL ETF.

timeline.title: SEC Announces Departure of David Saltiel
timeline.regulator: Securities and Exchange Commission — Division of Trading and Markets
timeline.one_line_why: Departure of a Division of Trading and Markets official creates review uncertainty but gives no clear directional signal on a spot Solana ETF approval.

timeline.title: Policy Statement Concerning Agency Referrals for Potential Criminal Enforcement
timeline.regulator: Securities and Exchange Commission — Division of Enforcement
timeline.one_line_why: Criminal-referral policy increases enforcement risk for crypto issuers, slightly lowering the odds the SEC will approve a spot Solana ETF in 2025.

timeline.title: Brian Daly Named Director of Division of Investment Management
timeline.regulator: Securities and Exchange Commission — Division of Investment Management
timeline.one_line_why: New Division of Investment Management director affects SEC fund oversight but gives no crypto/ETF stance, so it doesn't materially change spot SOL ETF odds.

timeline.title: Safeguarding Advisory Client Assets Investment Management
timeline.regulator: Securities and Exchange Commission — Division of Investment Management
timeline.one_line_why: SEC Investment Management guidance on safeguarding client assets is general and may only indirectly affect custody considerations for spot SOL ETFs.

timeline.title: SEC to Resume Processing of Registration Applications From Swiss-Based Investment Advisers
timeline.regulator: Securities and Exchange Commission
timeline.one_line_why: Resuming Swiss adviser registrations reduces regulatory friction for Swiss sponsors (e.g., 21Shares), slightly increasing chance of a U.S. spot SOL ETF approval.

timeline.title: Natasha Vij Greiner Will Conclude Her Tenure as Director of Investment Management
timeline.regulator: Securities and Exchange Commission — Division of Investment Management
timeline.one_line_why: SEC Investment Management director leaving is a background personnel change; it gives no clear directional signal on approval of a spot Solana ETF.

timeline.title: Form N-PORT and Form N-CEN Reporting; Guidance on Open-End Fund Liquidity Risk Investment Management
timeline.regulator: Securities and Exchange Commission — Division of Investment Management
timeline.one_line_why: General guidance on fund reporting and liquidity risk is background regulatory context for ETFs but does not directly affect a spot SOL ETF approval decision.

timeline.title: SEC Crypto Task Force Designation (PDF)
timeline.regulator: U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission — Office of the Chairman
timeline.one_line_why: SEC crypto task-force designation is background organizational news and does not directly change odds of a spot Solana ETF approval.

timeline.title: eToro Reaches Settlement with SEC and Will Cease Trading Activity in Nearly All Crypto Assets
timeline.regulator: Securities and Exchange Commission — Division of Enforcement
timeline.one_line_why: SEC settlement with eToro signals stricter enforcement toward crypto, lowering odds the SEC will approve a spot SOL ETF.

primary_drivers: SEC-CFTC joint statements and regulatory harmonization roundtables
Final rule delegating authority and other high-impact rulemakings
Custody and commodity-trust listing decisions plus Form PF compliance extension
heat_panel.explainer: Heat is critical because a concentrated run of high-impact SEC and CFTC actions — joint harmonization statements, roundtables, a delegation final rule, custody/listing decisions, and a Form PF extension — keeps regulatory scrutiny elevated.
